_source: Uniclass 2015 Table SL — Residential rooms (verbatim from primary NBS Source mirror declared in sprint-Z-source-provenance.md §1)
_note: Per-entry _verification_status records sourcing. Uniclass SL_45 (Residential spaces) contains a SINGLE sub-group SL_45_10 Living spaces with 21 verbatim leaf codes — confirmed on the NBS primary mirror taxon page for SL_45 (https://uniclass.thenbs.com/taxon/sl_45). SL does NOT enumerate sub-typed bedroom variants (master/secondary/guest), apartment dwelling-unit sizes (studio / 1-bed / 2-bed / 3-bed), or back-of-house residential spaces like mud rooms / boot rooms / snugs. Sub-typed canonical_ids share the closest NBS leaf code and are marked engineering_consensus with _inference_note citing BS 8233:2014 §7 (residential acoustic taxonomy), Approved Document Q 2015 (residential security room enumeration), and CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 (domestic occupancy categories) as engineering authority per sprint-Z-source-provenance.md §7 discipline. Additional Uniclass leaf codes recruited from sibling sub-groups where residential function justifies inclusion: SL_35_80 (Sanitary) for bathrooms/en-suites/showers/toilets; SL_90_10 (Circulation) for hallways/staircases/landings/lobbies/porches/vestibules/lifts; SL_90_20 (Common) for cloakrooms/communal lounges; SL_90_50 (Storage) for garages/carports/larders/wardrobes/locker rooms; SL_90_90 (Plant) for boiler rooms; SL_30_60 (Cleaning and maintenance) for laundries. cross_references.cibse_lg + nrm2 stay null per Sprint Z deferral; building_type_codes back-filled at Z.D.1.

entries._inference_note: NBS Source SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; sub-type differentiation (master vs secondary vs guest) is not enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35. Master designation follows BS 8233:2014 §7.7.1 dwelling acoustic taxonomy ('principal bedroom') and Approved Document Q 2015 secure-entry hierarchy.

NBS Source SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; sub-type differentiation per BS 8233:2014 §7.7.1 secondary-occupancy bedroom acoustic targets and CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 occupancy-density classifications.
NBS Source SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; guest sub-type per BS 8233:2014 §7.7 intermittent-occupancy acoustic class.

NBS Source SL_45_10_49 Living rooms is the closest verbatim leaf; family-room vs formal-living-room differentiation per BS 8233:2014 §7.7 dwelling spatial taxonomy (informal living space distinct from primary reception room).

NBS Source SL_45_10_49 Living rooms is the closest verbatim leaf; snug typology absent from SL v1.35 but enumerated as a distinct UK residential reception room class in BS 8233:2014 §7.7 (small intimate living space) and Approved Document Q 2015 (secondary social room).

NBS Source SL_42_40_79 Snooker/billiards/pool halls covers commercial venues; domestic games room (table tennis, billiards, console gaming) is not enumerated in SL v1.35 residential leaves. SL_45_10_49 Living rooms used as the closest residential parent per BS 8233:2014 §7.7 (residential recreational room class).

NBS Source SL_40_60_40 Indoor theatre spaces covers commercial venues; domestic media/home-cinema room not enumerated as a residential leaf in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_49 Living rooms used as the closest residential parent. CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 acknowledges 'media room' as a distinct domestic acoustic-treatment class.

NBS Source SL_45_10_93 Utility rooms is the closest verbatim leaf; UK boot-room typology (rear-entry transitional space for outdoor clothing/footwear) not separately enumerated in SL v1.35 but distinguished from utility room in BS 8233:2014 dwelling acoustic taxonomy and CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 (intermediate-temperature ancillary spaces).

US residential typology equivalent to UK boot room; not enumerated as a separate residential leaf in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_93 Utility rooms used as the closest verbatim parent per CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 ancillary-space classification.

SL_45_10_94 Verandas is the closest residential outdoor leaf; patio (unroofed paved garden space) not enumerated separately in SL v1.35. BS 8233:2014 §7.7 acknowledges patio as a distinct external residential amenity space.
SL_45_10_94 Verandas is the closest residential outdoor leaf; deck (raised timber external platform, common in US/AU residential) not enumerated separately in SL v1.35.

NBS Source SL_35_80_08 Bathrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; master-bathroom designation per BS 8233:2014 §7.7.2 sanitary taxonomy and BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701 special-locations zoning (Zone 0/1/2 applicable in all bathroom variants).
NBS Source SL_35_80_08 Bathrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; secondary differentiation per BS 8233:2014 §7.7.2 sanitary taxonomy.

NBS Source SL_35_80_08 Bathrooms is the verbatim parent leaf; en-suite (bathroom directly attached to a bedroom, no corridor access) is a distinct UK residential adjacency class per BS 8233:2014 §7.7.2 sanitary taxonomy and BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701 zoning rules.
NBS Source SL_35_80_89 Toilets is the verbatim parent leaf; powder-room/half-bath typology (WC + basin without bath/shower) per BS 8233:2014 §7.7.2 sanitary taxonomy.

Basement/cellar not enumerated as a discrete residential leaf in SL v1.35. SL_90_50_84 Storage rooms used as the closest functional verbatim parent; basement designation per CIBSE Guide A 2015 §1.A2 below-ground habitable-space class and BS 8233:2014 §7.7 dwelling typology.

Attic/loft not enumerated as a discrete residential leaf in SL v1.35. SL_90_50_84 Storage rooms used as the closest functional verbatim parent for non-habitable storage attics; habitable loft conversions inherit SL_45_10_09 bedroom mapping.

Dwelling-unit-size classifications (studio / 1-bed / 2-bed) are NOT enumerated in Uniclass SL v1.35 — SL classifies rooms, not whole-dwelling units. SL_45_10_45 Kitchen-dining-living rooms is the closest functional verbatim leaf for the studio single-room dwelling pattern. Approved Document Q 2015 and the Nationally Described Space Standard 2015 enumerate studio (1p), 1-bed (2p), 2-bed, 3-bed thresholds.

Dwelling-unit-size classifications not in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the closest verbatim leaf for unit-defining bedroom count. Per Nationally Described Space Standard 2015 1-bed/2-person threshold.
Dwelling-unit-size classifications not in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the closest verbatim leaf. Per Nationally Described Space Standard 2015 2-bed/3-person and 2-bed/4-person thresholds.
Dwelling-unit-size classifications not in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms is the closest verbatim leaf. Per Nationally Described Space Standard 2015 3-bed thresholds (4-, 5-, 6-person variants).

Penthouse (top-floor luxury dwelling unit) not enumerated in SL v1.35. SL_45_10_09 Bedrooms used as the closest verbatim leaf for the unit-defining bedroom; BS 8233:2014 §7.7 acknowledges penthouse as a distinct residential adjacency class (no neighbours above).
